title: Formally Certified Manipulation Evidence via Causal-Bayesian Inference and Adversarial Evaluation

reviewers.name: Yuan Cheng
reviewers.key_strength: Exact Bayesian inference via arithmetic circuit compilation on bounded-treewidth LOB models eliminates approximation error that makes MCMC/variational posteriors unsuitable for evidentiary claims.
reviewers.key_weakness: Bayes factors are sensitive to prior specification on manipulation parameters (Jeffreys-Lindley paradox), and no prior sensitivity analysis or robust evidence measures are specified.

reviewers.name: Aniruddha Sinha
reviewers.key_strength: The FO-MTL monitoring layer with O(1) amortized event processing and dual-solver verification via Z3/CVC5 is a well-designed quantitative extension of existing monitoring frameworks.
reviewers.key_weakness: The adversarial RL strategy-space coverage bound depends critically on the strategy parameterization, which is not specified in sufficient detail to evaluate the stress-test's utility.

reviewers.name: Lila Zhang
reviewers.key_strength: The probabilistic-to-logical proof bridge with equisatisfiability guarantee is a genuinely novel contribution at the intersection of Bayesian reasoning and automated theorem proving.
reviewers.key_weakness: The 22K LoC SMT encoding layer is itself unverified and becomes the weakest TCB link; without independent proof checking (LFSC/Lean) the formal guarantee rests on unverified code.

reviewers.name: Sara Roy
reviewers.key_strength: Theorem M7.4's quantitative TV bounds on posterior degradation under DAG misspecification extends prior work (Uhler et al., Peters-Bühlmann) to the posterior-over-effects setting with sample-size dependence.
reviewers.key_weakness: Sim-to-real calibration using marginal KS-statistics does not guarantee joint distributional fidelity, risking RL evasion strategies that exploit simulator artifacts rather than real detection blind spots.

reviewers.name: Joseph S. Chang
reviewers.key_strength: The stratified proof architecture with measure-theoretic composition framework (M5.3) correctly formalizes cross-framework evidence composition as assume-guarantee reasoning with verifiable compatibility conditions.
reviewers.key_weakness: Dual-solver verification (Z3+CVC5) provides pragmatic but not principled TCB reduction due to architectural similarity; independent proof checking via LFSC or Lean formalization would be stronger.

consensus_strengths: The three-way integration of causal identification, exact Bayesian inference, and SMT-certified compliance verification produces evidence artifacts with properties no existing system achieves even pairwise.
The probabilistic-to-logical proof bridge is a genuinely novel formalization that faithfully embeds Bayesian reasoning in QF_LRA without reducing to naive posterior thresholding.

Arithmetic circuit compilation for exact inference on bounded-treewidth LOB models eliminates approximation error at the foundation, ensuring evidence strength claims are mathematically exact given the model.

Honest scoping of non-claims (not a legal evidence system, not exhaustive adversarial coverage, not assumption-free causal claims) demonstrates intellectual maturity and pre-empts predictable reviewer objections.
consensus_weaknesses: The 22K LoC SMT proof generation layer is the largest unverified component in the TCB and represents the primary soundness risk; no independent proof checking or formal verification of the encoding is planned.
Bayes factor sensitivity to prior specification is unaddressed, creating vulnerability to adversarial critiques of evidence strength in any downstream regulatory application.
Sim-to-real calibration relies on marginal distributional matching (KS-statistics) without validating joint dependence structure, limiting the credibility of RL-discovered evasion strategies.
Theorem M7.4's tightness target (non-vacuous for k ≤ 3 edge misspecifications) may be insufficient for practical financial time series where regime shifts cause larger structural changes.
The strategy-space parameterization for adversarial RL stress-testing is underspecified, making the coverage bound's practical meaning difficult to evaluate.
actionable_recommendations: Implement independent proof checking via CVC5's LFSC proof format or translate Z3 proof objects to Lean 4 terms, reducing TCB dependence on the 22K LoC encoding layer.
Report Bayes factors under multiple prior specifications (reference, empirical Bayes, adversarial) and provide minimum Bayes factor over a prior class as a robust evidence measure.
Extend sim-to-real calibration beyond marginal KS-statistics to include multivariate dependence measures (copula structure, tail dependence coefficients, cross-correlation decay).
Provide degradation curves for Theorem M7.4 beyond k=3, characterizing the regime where formal guarantees become vacuous and specifying operational constraints for the system.
Formally verify the SMT encoding's correctness by implementing it in Lean 4 with extracted verified code, or prove a meta-theorem establishing encoding soundness.
